stem:
Match the following pairs:
A. Number of distinct binary trees with n nodes
B. Number of binary strings of length 2n with an equal number of 0s and 1s
C. Number of even permutations of n objects
D. Number of binary strings of length 2n which are palindromes

P. C(2n, n)
Q. C(2n, n) / (n+1)
R. n! / 2
S. (1/(n+1)) * C(2n, n)

Which of the following is the correct matching?
reason: Parse misidentified left-column items (A-D) as answer options and merged right-column items plus matching options into option D text. Actual answer options are the matching combinations A-Q B-P C-R D-S etc.
correctOptions incorrectly contains ["D", "F"]; sourceAnswer is "D. F(n+2)" where F is part of the option text (Fibonacci notation), not a second correct key.

stem: Let R and S be any two equivalence relations on a non-empty set A. Which one of the following statements is TRUE?

options.text: R union S and R intersect S are both equivalence relations
R union S is an equivalence relation
R intersect S is an equivalence relation
Neither R union S nor R intersect S are equivalence relations

reason: Stem truncated (missing 'A.' at end); option A text incorrectly prepended with 'Which one of the following statements is TRUE? A. ' — that text belongs in the stem.
